channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Zanzibar targets regional tech hub status through innovation investments
item.description: Zanzibar is positioning itself as a regional technology hub by focusing on innovation and infrastructure development. The initiative aims to attract investments, foster tech startups, and create emplo...

item.title: Heavy rainfall triggers floods and landslides, killing 16 in J&K, Uttarakhand, Nagaland
item.description: Sixteen people were killed across Jammu and Kashmir (J&K), Uttarakhand, and Nagaland due to heavy rainfall that triggered flash floods and landslides. J&K was the worst-affected region, with 11 deaths...

item.title: Kapseret MP Urges Tough Action Against Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua
item.description: Kapseret Member of Parliament Oscar Sudi has publicly demanded that President William Ruto take strict measures against former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua, accusing him of deepening political di...

item.title: Lee Andrews Allegedly Plans to Sell Sex Tape with Katie Price for £250,000
item.description: Lee Andrews, a self-proclaimed millionaire businessman, has allegedly been discussing the sale of a purported sex tape with his wife Katie Price for months, with claims he aims to sell the footage to ...

item.title: Nauru detainees struggle with inadequate support and rising costs
item.description: Community detainees in Nauru are facing severe hardships due to insufficient financial support from the Australian government. A report by the Asylum Seeker Resource Centre reveals that 99% of 78 inte...

item.title: DFA's Lazaro to discuss bilateral relations with China's foreign minister
item.description: Foreign Affairs Secretary Ma. Theresa Lazaro is set to meet China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi to address bilateral relations between the Philippines and China during ASEAN-related meetings in Metro Man...

item.title: Oyo Teacher's 56-Day Captivity: Kidnappers Released Them Before Security Intervention
item.description: A teacher rescued after 56 days in captivity in Oyo State has revealed that kidnappers released him and other victims before security forces intervened. Zacchaeus Olatunde recounted harsh conditions d...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Escalating US-Iran Tensions in Middle East
item.description: Brent crude oil prices surged past $90 per barrel as escalating tensions between the U.S. and Iran disrupted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. The conflict, which intensified over the weeken...

item.title: Security Forces Heighten Operations in BARMM Amid Election Violence Fears
item.description: Security forces in the Bangsamoro region have intensified operations as the autonomous region begins its first parliamentary election period, amid fears of election-related violence spilling over into...

item.title: The Spectator's Islamophobic Coverage and Its Impact on British Media
item.description: Nesrine Malik critiques the Spectator, Britain's oldest political magazine, for its systematic promotion of Islamophobia. A comprehensive report by the Centre for Media Monitoring analysed 3,733 artic...

item.title: Oshwal Academy: Details on Fees, Curriculum, Location, and Parent Resources
item.description: Oshwal Academy is a leading international school in Kenya with branches in Nairobi and Mombasa, offering the British National Curriculum. The school caters to learners aged 1.5 to 18, divided into kin...

item.title: Cameroon's Gold Exports: Challenges in Tracking Illicit Trade
item.description: The article investigates the significant issue of Cameroon's gold exports, revealing that only about 5% of the country's gold production is legally exported. The majority of the gold leaves the countr...

item.title: Dr. Toyosi Akerele-Ogunsiji urges governments to invest in AI education and research
item.description: Dr. Toyosi Akerele-Ogunsiji, an AI governance expert, urged governments to invest more in universities and equip lecturers with tools to drive technological innovation. She emphasized that AI will tra...

item.title: Spain's Defensive Mastery Secures Second World Cup Title
item.description: Spain's historic second FIFA World Cup victory over Argentina in the 2026 tournament was celebrated with emphasis on their unparalleled defensive record. Coach Luis de la Fuente's team conceded just o...

item.title: Akinwumi Adesina's Continued Leadership in African Development Initiatives
item.description: Akinwumi Adesina, the former president of the African Development Bank (AfDB), has swiftly transitioned into new leadership roles after leaving his position. Just months post-AfDB, he assumed the chai...

item.title: Alfred Meyer Challenges New York Times' Radiation Safety Narrative
item.description: Alfred Meyer, a prominent figure in Physicians for Social Responsibility, criticizes a New York Times article that advocates for relaxing radiation safety regulations to boost nuclear energy expansion...

item.title: CJP and Government Talks Remain Unclear Amid Hunger Strike Conditions

item.title: Tate Brothers Face Extradition Charges in UK Rape and Trafficking Case
item.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate, the controversial influencer brothers known for their misogynistic rhetoric, were arrested in Miami by U.S. Marshals on a sealed warrant related to extradition requests from B...

item.title: Kenyans Turn Football Screening into Political Protest at KICC
item.description: Hundreds of Kenyans gathered at Nairobi's KICC for a public screening of the Spain vs Argentina World Cup match, creating a vibrant atmosphere. However, the event took an unexpected political turn as ...

item.title: Coco Martin and Dingdong Dantes Announce New AKTOR PH Project
item.description: Coco Martin and Dingdong Dantes have joined forces for a new project under the AKTOR PH initiative, marking a significant collaboration in Philippine entertainment. The partnership, announced on July ...
